scenario_results.reason: The package handled hazardous-waste inspection recovery inside operator controls and evidence discipline without claiming final legal sufficiency.
TRI readiness work structured owners, missing data, and review steps without inventing unsupported threshold calls.
EPCRA readiness stayed inside inventory and evidence organization while escalating unresolved legal or threshold questions.
SPCC support remained bounded to inspections and corrective actions rather than engineering redesign.
Pretreatment obligations were framed as evidence and routing work rather than final permit compliance statements.
Process-safety engineering ownership was rejected clearly and routed to the proper technical lane.
Low-confidence handling requested missing inventories and release data instead of fabricating applicability conclusions.
Authority-expectation review was routed outward rather than collapsed into internal self-certification.
The executive summary remained concise, plant-usable, and explicit about assumptions and routed next steps.
Product stewardship work was rejected to preserve the chemicals environmental compliance boundary.

notes: All 10 scenarios passed against a strict 0.92 threshold for chemicals-sector operator-side environmental compliance support.
The package cleanly separates chemicals environmental compliance from process-safety engineering, permitting authority, and product stewardship.
